# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
- name: projectsService
  type: googleapis.com/googlecloudpubsub/v1/projects
  properties:
    serviceAccountEmail: ${SA_EMAIL}
    projectId: ${PROJECT_ID}

- name: computeInstanceTemplate
  type: googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/zone/${ZONE}/instanceTemplates
  properties:
    instanceTemplate:
      disks:
        - boot: true
          autoDetect: true
          encryptionKey: 'projects/my-encryption-key-project-id/locations/global/keys/my-encryption-key'
      machineType: ${INSTANCE_TYPE}
      serviceAccounts:
        - 5821960347773509827 # Service account for default Compute Engine service
      name: my-instance-template
      tags:
        - http-server

- name: computeInstance
  type: googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/zones/${ZONE}/instances
  properties:
    instance:
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          source: ${COMPUTE_INSTANCE_TEMPLATE}
      name: my-instance-${INSTANCE_NUMBER}
      machineType: ${INSTANCE_TYPE}
      canIpForward: true
      metadata:
        items:
          - key: startup-script
            value: |
              #!/bin/bash
              apt-get update
              apt-get install -y apache2
      serviceAccounts:
        - 5821960347773509827 # Service account for default Compute Engine service
    deploymentConfig: ${DEPLOYMENT_CONFIG}
    zone: ${ZONE}
